action.hypothesis: Round 28 showed that `whitened_topk` is not worth continuing: under a matched probe it failed catastrophically, so the next and only remaining useful question in this probe slot is whether a different directly compatible support-selection variant has any architectural merit at all. `gated` preserves the same static-dictionary weighted-sum reconstruction form as `topk` while changing only how support is selected and scaled, so this round asks a single yes/no question: is `gated` competitive enough under a matched K128/signum/LR=1.6e-3/EF12 probe to justify any future follow-up.
action.summary: Run one matched architecture probe on `gated`. Keep `K=128`, `OPTIMIZER=signum`, `LR=1.6e-3`, `EXPANSION_FACTOR=12`, and the current stabilized runtime/aux recipe fixed, and change only `ARCHITECTURE` from `topk` to `gated` to test whether this directly export-compatible family is worth continuing.
action.self_review: This obeys the matched-probe rule by changing only the architecture axis, keeps the core mainline recipe fixed, and selects a family that remains compatible with LUTurbo's static-vector weighted-sum deployment contract. The main risk is that the extra gating branch may add training complexity without improving quality or runtime-aligned sparsity behavior, but that is exactly the family-worthiness question this probe is intended to answer before returning to the topk mainline.
notes_to_memory: Round 28 ruled out `whitened_topk` as a promising matched probe candidate because it was massively worse than the topk control.

`gated` remains directly compatible with LUTurbo deployment because the final reconstruction is still a finite weighted sum over a static decoder library with fixed `K`; the change is confined to encoder-side gating and support scoring.

Judge this round as a pure family-worthiness probe against the stabilized K128 topk control: if it is clearly worse or unstable, archive it and return to topk next round; if it is competitive, record that but still return to the mainline per policy.
next_hypotheses: If `gated` is clearly worse than matched `topk`, archive it immediately and return to the topk mainline next round.
If `gated` is competitive but not clearly better, record it as a plausible alternative family and still return to the topk mainline next round.
If `gated` beats the matched `topk` control, schedule at most one later follow-up after the mandated return to the mainline.

The main risk is that the extra gating branch may add training complexity without improving quality or runtime-aligned sparsity behavior, but that is exactly the family-worthiness question this probe is intended to answer before returning to the topk mainline.
